mechanism: Fluid movement (magma, hydrothermal water, volcanic gases) through volcanic conduits within Mt. Rainier's edifice. Conduit resonance produces sustained infrasonic tremor. Harmonic tremor episodes associated with increased magmatic activity.
status: GEOPHYSICS ARC OPENS at degree 261. After the Storm Arc maximum (E=9 at degree 260), the engine descends underground. The Geophysics Arc documents the volcanic, seismic, and hydrological forces operating beneath the PNW landscape at infrasonic and subsonic frequencies.

mountain_data.name: Mt. Rainier / Tahoma
mountain_data.indigenous_name: Tahoma (Lushootseed/Coast Salish: 'the mother of all waters')
mountain_data.named_by: George Vancouver, 1792, after Rear Admiral Peter Rainier

tremor_physics.mechanism: Fluid movement through conduits causes conduit wall vibration; sustained resonance analogous to pipe organ
tremor_physics.harmonic_tremor: Episodes with harmonic frequency structure (fundamental + overtones), associated with increased magmatic activity
tremor_physics.primary_source: Hydrothermal system: hot springs, fumaroles, ice cave network at summit
tremor_physics.detection: Seismometers and infrasonic pressure sensors; not detectable by human ear or conventional microphone

hazard_assessment.usgs_classification: Highest priority volcanic threat (by some assessments, most dangerous in USA)
infrastructure_at_risk: Seattle-Tacoma International Airport (near White River pathway)
hazard_assessment.orting_warning_minutes: 30-45 (insufficient for vehicle evacuation in modern traffic conditions)
hazard_assessment.osceola_mudflow: 5,600 years ago; reached Puget Sound; documented connection to SPS degree 262 (lahar)

monitoring.dual_function: Tremor monitoring network = early warning system. Same instruments that track background activity provide eruption/lahar warning.

theory_nodes.concept: GEOPHYSICS ARC OPENS at degree 261. After Storm Arc (256-260) at surface, engine descends underground. Geophysics Arc documents volcanic (261-262), seismic (263), and hydrological (264) forces operating at infrasonic/subsonic frequencies. The underground machinery of the PNW is older than the surface and operates continuously.
theory_nodes.description: Geophysics Arc opens: underground forces after surface storm maximum.

theory_nodes.concept: Volcanic tremor as infrasonic communication. Mt. Rainier produces infrasonic signals (below 1-10 Hz) continuously. Infrasound propagates farther with less attenuation than audible sound. The mountain's background voice is detectable at distances of hundreds of kilometers. The SPS engine documents this at E=5 -- the steady-state acoustic output of an active stratovolcano.
theory_nodes.description: Infrasonic volcanic tremor: the mountain's continuous background voice.

theory_nodes.concept: Tahoma as most dangerous US volcano: hazard + exposure combination. 26 glaciers (~35 sq mi) as lahar multiplier. Puyallup, White, Carbon River valleys as lahar pathways through urban Puget Sound corridor. Orting warning time: 30-45 minutes. The tremor monitoring network IS the early warning system.
theory_nodes.description: Mt. Rainier volcanic threat: hazard mechanics and urban exposure.

theory_nodes.concept: Hydrothermal system as primary tremor source. Summit fumaroles, hot springs, ice cave network (caves melted by sustained thermal emission beneath glacier ice). Hydrothermal alteration of rock = edifice weakening. Large-scale edifice collapse possible without major eruption. Connects to Osceola Mudflow (5,600 years ago, reached Puget Sound) documented at SPS degree 262.
theory_nodes.description: Hydrothermal system: the source of tremor and the source of collapse risk.

cross_references.relationship: TRANSITION: Storm Arc maximum (E=9) to Geophysics Arc opening (E=5). Surface to underground. The engine descends.
VOLCANIC SEQUENCE continues. Tremor (261) to lahar (262). The mountain's ongoing activity to the mountain's destructive potential. Osceola Mudflow thread.

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=5 + E=7. Both operate below the ordinary register: Lanegan's baritone below the usual vocal range; volcanic tremor below the usual acoustic range. Underground voice and underground force. The Geophysics Arc opens with a pairing of two below-surface energies.

milestones: STORM ARC TO GEOPHYSICS ARC TRANSITION
